edge_hypothesis: SOL MOMENTUM CASCADE — MACD ON A NEW ASSET AND TIMEFRAME: MACD confirmation has 9 ACCEPTs — the most consistent template — but ALL 9 are on ETH. SOL has never been backtested with MACD (or any template). SOL has fundamentally different market microstructure: higher beta (~1.5x ETH), more impulsive directional moves, wider spreads, and a younger/more retail-driven order book. MACD histogram zero-cross is a momentum ignition signal — when the histogram crosses zero, it signals the start of a new momentum wave. On SOL, these momentum waves tend to be LARGER and MORE DECISIVE than on ETH because SOL's price action is more impulsive and less mean-reverting. The EMA50 confirmation (close > EMA50 for longs) ensures we're trading with the medium-term trend, not counter-trend. At 8:1 R:R on SOL/4h, we test the core question: does MACD's proven alpha on ETH transfer to SOL? Additionally, the 1h timeframe variant tests whether MACD's alpha is timeframe-specific. On 1h, MACD captures intraday momentum cascades — the rapid price moves when institutional flow hits SOL's thinner order book. 4x more bars = 4x more potential signals = better statistical power. Invalidation: if SOL PF < 1.0 across both timeframes, MACD's alpha is ETH-specific and the signal logic doesn't generalize.

variants.name: macd_sol_momentum_8to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross + close > EMA50 on SOL/4h with 8:1 R:R. First MACD test on SOL. SOL's impulsive price action should produce cleaner momentum ignition signals. 2.0x ATR stop, 16.0x ATR TP. Expected ~100-160 trades based on ETH trade count at similar R:R. If SOL PF > ETH MACD PF (1.460 at 6:1), SOL is a superior asset for momentum strategies.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from negative to positive (histogram zero-cross, bullish momentum ignition).
Close > EMA_50 (trading with medium-term trend confirmation).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from positive to negative (histogram zero-cross, bearish momentum ignition).
Close < EMA_50 (trading with medium-term trend confirmation).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (16.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0 on SOL, MACD's alpha is ETH-specific — do not expand to SOL
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 16.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend filter — close must be above/below for directional confirmation
MACDh_12_26_9 zero-cross — momentum ignition signal on SOL
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 16.0x TP — 8:1 R:R on SOL's higher-beta price action

variants.name: macd_sol_1h_rapid_7to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross + close > EMA50 on SOL/1h with 7:1 R:R. The 1h timeframe captures SOL's intraday momentum cascades — rapid directional moves when institutional flow hits the order book. MACD at 1h produces ~4x more signals than 4h. At 7:1 R:R (the proven sweet spot for MACD on ETH), this should yield 300-500 trades for robust statistical validation. 2.0x ATR stop, 14.0x ATR TP.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from negative to positive (histogram zero-cross, bullish momentum ignition).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from positive to negative (histogram zero-cross, bearish momentum ignition).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (14.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < SOL/4h variant, MACD degrades on faster timeframes for SOL — stick to 4h
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 14.0x ATR_14 from entry.

components.notes: EMA_50 trend filter on SOL/1h — intraday trend alignment
MACDh_12_26_9 zero-cross — captures intraday momentum cascades on SOL
ATR_14 for 2.0x stop / 14.0x TP — 7:1 R:R proven sweet spot at higher frequency

variants.name: macd_sol_extreme_10to1
variants.description: MACD histogram zero-cross + close > EMA50 on SOL/4h with 10:1 R:R. The extreme R:R variant. MACD 12:1 on ETH achieved PF=1.302 and the remarkable trending PF=2.177 — the only MACD variant profitable in trending. On SOL, which has more extreme directional moves, 10:1 R:R should capture larger tails. This variant tests whether SOL's impulsive character allows even wider TP targets. If PF > ETH MACD at comparable R:R, SOL becomes the preferred asset for extreme tail harvesting.
entry_long: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from negative to positive (histogram zero-cross, bullish momentum ignition).
entry_short: MACDh_12_26_9 crosses from positive to negative (histogram zero-cross, bearish momentum ignition).
filters: Backtester executable indicators: EMA, SMA, RSI, ATR, MACD, Bollinger Bands, Stochastic, ADX, CCI, Williams %R, OBV, VWAP, Ichimoku, Supertrend, Donchian Channels.
exit_rules: Exit on ATR-based take profit hit (20.0x ATR).
Exit on ATR-based stop loss hit (2.0x ATR).
Hypothesis failure mode: if PF < 1.0, SOL's noise-to-signal ratio at 10:1 R:R is too high — stick to 8:1
risk_rules: Stop loss at 2.0x ATR_14 below/above entry.
Take profit at 20.0x ATR_14 from entry.

MACDh_12_26_9 zero-cross — momentum ignition for SOL's impulsive moves
